title: Seizure
warning: Stabilize ABCs and check glucose early. Time seizure duration and medication doses.

entry.title: Active seizure?
entry.prompt: Currently seizing, recurrent without baseline, or seizure >5 minutes?

status.title: Status epilepticus
actions: Labs: glucose, electrolytes, ASM levels if relevant

recovered.title: Recovered seizure
recovered.prompt: Simple febrile, known epilepsy, or concerning/first seizure?

simple.title: Simple febrile seizure
simple.prompt: Generalized, <15 min, once in 24h, age 6mo-5y, baseline.
actions: No routine labs/imaging/EEG solely for simple febrile seizure

complex.title: Complex/concerning seizure
actions: Neuroimaging if trauma/focal deficit/ICP signs/persistent AMS

discharge.title: Discharge
dispositionCriteria: Return for prolonged/recurrent seizure, persistent AMS, neck stiffness, respiratory issues, trauma

admit.title: Admit/observe
actions: Handoff seizure duration, meds/timing, glucose, neuro exam, pending studies

title: Head Injury
warning: Use validated head injury rules and judgment. Consider NAT when history/exam mismatch.

entry.title: Primary survey
entry.prompt: GCS abnormal, skull fracture signs, seizure, persistent AMS, focal neuro deficit, or unstable trauma?

under2.title: <2 years features
under2.prompt: Scalp hematoma except frontal, LOC >=5 sec, severe mechanism, not acting normally?
actions: Screen for abuse if inconsistent history/bruising

over2.title: >=2 years features
over2.prompt: Vomiting, LOC, severe headache, severe mechanism?

observe.title: Observation vs CT
observe.prompt: Shared decision based on worsening symptoms, multiple findings, age, reliability.
actions: CT if worsening/multiple risk factors/unreliable observation/clinician concern

concussion.title: Concussion
concussion.prompt: No red flags; manage symptoms and return-to-activity.

nat.title: Possible abuse
nat.prompt: Protect child and broaden injury evaluation.
actions: Skeletal survey/head imaging/labs per age/local pathway

discharge.title: Discharge
dispositionCriteria: Return for worsening headache, repeated vomiting, confusion, seizure, weakness, abnormal behavior, difficult to wake

admit.title: Admit/observe
admit.prompt: Needs serial exams or specialty care
actions: Handoff neuro trend, imaging, meds, consults

title: Limp / Refusal to Walk
warning: Local ortho/rheum/ID pathways should guide definitive management.

entry.title: Danger screen
entry.prompt: Fever, toxic appearance, severe pain, non-weight-bearing, neuro deficit, trauma concern, or unclear mechanism in young child?
actions: Expose and examine hips to toes

red.title: Red flag limp
red.prompt: Differentiate infection, fracture, malignancy, hip emergency.
actions: X-ray area of pain; include hip if unclear
Ortho consult if septic joint possible

localize.title: Localized vs transient
localize.prompt: Well appearing child with mild limp.

septic.title: Septic arthritis/osteomyelitis
actions: Ortho consult before antibiotics if stable and aspiration planned
Antibiotics promptly if septic/toxic or after cultures/aspiration per plan

fracture.title: Fracture/occult injury
fracture.prompt: Toddler fracture can be radiographically occult.
actions: Immobilize if clinical suspicion despite negative x-ray
Assess developmental mechanism and abuse risk

scfe.title: SCFE/hip emergency
scfe.prompt: Adolescent hip, groin, thigh, or knee pain with limp.
actions: AP pelvis/frog-leg lateral unless unstable slip concern

synovitis.title: Transient synovitis likely
synovitis.prompt: Diagnosis of exclusion in well child.

nat.title: Possible nonaccidental trauma
nat.prompt: Mechanism mismatch or concerning bruising/fracture pattern.
actions: Skeletal survey/labs/head imaging by age/local pathway

discharge.title: Discharge
dispositionCriteria: Return for fever, worsening pain, inability to bear weight, swelling/redness, night pain

admit.title: Admit/consult
admit.prompt: Needs inpatient or urgent specialty care
actions: Handoff weight-bearing status, imaging, labs, antibiotics timing, consult recommendations
